scenarios.query:
Audit this dependency scan. Project: Apache-2.0 SaaS, also shipped on-prem to customers.
```text
package        version  pinned?  cves           last release  maintainers  license   notes
requets        2.31     no       -              5 years ago   1            MIT       name 1 char off `requests`
img-resize     3.0.1    lock     CVE-2026-114   3 weeks ago   5            MIT       fix in 3.0.2
license-check  1.2.0    lock     -              2 months ago  2            GPL-3.0   imported as a library at runtime
```
expected_behavior: Flags the typosquat (requets vs requests) as a likely malicious/mistaken package needing immediate verification, and its unpinned floating version
Flags the known CVE with the available fixed version (bump img-resize to 3.0.2)
Flags the GPL-3.0 runtime library inside an Apache-2.0 product shipped on-prem as a license-compatibility risk to escalate

scenarios.query:
Audit this scan of a new PR adding one dependency.
```text
package      version  pinned?            cves  last release  maintainers  weekly downloads  notes
is-positive  ^2.0     no lockfile entry  -     7 years ago   1            120               adds 3 transitive deps; replaces `n > 0`
```
expected_behavior: Flags the trivial dependency (replaces a one-line expression) and recommends removing it in favor of inline code
Flags the unpinned version / missing lockfile entry
Flags the abandonment/single-maintainer signals as supply-chain surface not worth the cost for zero benefit

scenarios.query:
Audit this dependency scan. Project: MIT web service.
```text
package   version  pinned?  cves  last release  maintainers  license  notes
express   4.21.0   lock     -     1 month ago   20+          MIT      renovate enabled, npm ci in CI
pg        8.13.1   lock     -     2 months ago  10           MIT      -
```
expected_behavior: Reports no findings (dependencies and supply chain are healthy)
Recognizes pinned lockfile usage enforced in CI, active maintenance, clean scans, compatible licenses
Does not invent risks or recommend alternative libraries as a matter of preference
